description: Behavior cases for mobile/responsive viewport craft: content-driven breakpoints, touch-target sizing, the 100vh/dynamic-viewport-unit fix, hover-absence handling, keyboard behavior, and wide-table reflow.

cases.prompt: Our app was built desktop-first and it's rough on phones — things overflow sideways and buttons are hard to tap. Design the responsive/touch craft.

assertions: Adopts a mobile-first approach with a stated minimum width and content-driven breakpoints (where the layout breaks), not a list of device widths.
Sets touch-target minimums (~44px) with spacing and ensures the tappable area matches the visual, placing primaries in the thumb zone.
Diagnoses horizontal overflow and prescribes reflow patterns rather than horizontal scrolling of the whole page.
Names the boundary: page weight/network cost is handed to frontend-perf-engineer.

cases.prompt: On mobile our full-height drawer gets cut off at the bottom and the sticky footer sits under the phone's home bar. Fix it.

assertions: Identifies the 100vh trap: 100vh includes the space the collapsing URL bar reclaims, so the bottom is clipped — prescribes dynamic viewport units (dvh/svh/lvh).
Applies safe-area-inset for the notch and home indicator so fixed elements clear system UI.
Checks that fixed headers/footers don't collide with the on-screen keyboard.
Treats this as a correctness bug, not a cosmetic tweak.

cases.prompt: Our table row actions and tooltips only appear on hover. They're completely inaccessible on touch devices. Redesign the interactions.

assertions: Recognizes touch has no hover event, so hover-only affordances simply don't exist for touch users.
Provides touch-reachable equivalents (visible action buttons, tap-to-disclose, an explicit menu) rather than relying on hover.
Ensures any custom gestures are discoverable and don't conflict with browser gestures (back-swipe, pull-to-refresh).
Notes a11y intent for the new controls and hands verification to accessibility-test-harness.

cases.prompt: We have a 12-column data table that's unusable on a phone. How should it work on small screens?

assertions: Chooses a deliberate wide-table pattern (stack-to-cards, horizontal scroll with a frozen key column, or priority-column collapse) and states the tradeoff.
Avoids hiding critical columns/actions purely to fit the width.
Keeps the tappable controls within touch-size guidelines in the chosen layout.
Does not simply shrink the desktop table to illegibility.

cases.prompt: Our mobile bundle is 2.5MB and takes 6 seconds to become interactive on a mid-range Android. Cut the weight and improve the load metrics.

assertions: Does not invoke mobile-viewport-craft: bundle weight, time-to-interactive, and load metrics on low-end devices are frontend-perf-engineer.
Viewport craft concerns layout/touch/viewport correctness, not payload size or JS execution cost.
